FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Fintech and legacy players both chasing banking charters while regional M&A ticks up and Fed officials brawl over rate cuts.

bullets.text: Klarna applies for ILC charter—Swedish fintech making regulatory play to take deposits and lend directly in the U.S.
Sony gets OCC conditional nod for trust charter to launch stablecoin manager Connectia in 2027—another nonbank eyeing regulated banking services.
Regional M&A moving: Hometown Bank (MA) buying NH shop for $160M; Bank7 pursuing 71% of Century Bank via forced shareholder asset sale.
Fed minutes will expose rate-cut divisions among officials—hawks vs. doves clash could stretch the debate across multiple meetings.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: RIA market consolidation accelerates with Choreo and Wavvest acquisitions, while Goldman pushes into custody and Fisher Investments defies M&A trend with organic $300B growth.

bullets.text: Wavvest snags $1.4B advisor Vinnie Maliakkal from Schwab as managing director—AI-native platform making a statement hire after 11 years of advisor tenure.
Choreo continues consolidation spree by acquiring $700M planning-focused RIA under new CEO Jason Van de Loo, signaling appetite for bolt-on deals.
Fisher Investments grew to ~$450B AUM organically since 2019 without M&A, adding $88B+ in last 18 months through media blitz—defying industry consolidation trend.
Goldman Sachs gaining traction in RIA custody via brand recognition and UHNW focus, though still a small player behind Schwab, Fidelity, E*TRADE in consolidating market.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Reinsurance capacity tightening amid MGA expansion, while Middle East shipping risks spike insurance claims exposure.

bullets.text: Gallagher Re's ceded premiums up 18% YoY—MGA market expansion driving sustained reinsurance capacity demand.
RGA doubles down on Japan with two life reinsurance coinsurance deals (Sumitomo Life, Tokio Marine), signaling Asia-Pacific growth strategy.
Qatari LNG tanker struck off Oman; Iran targeting additional vessels—Hormuz escalation creating shipping disruption and insurance claims pipeline.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Big payments consolidation, Mexico expansion, and infrastructure shifts dominate—JPMorgan/BofA eye Fiserv debit network while capital rotates toward AI data centers.

bullets.text: JPMorgan and BofA in active talks to acquire Fiserv's debit payments network; no valuation disclosed yet but signals continued mega-bank appetite for payments infrastructure.
Equifax paying $750M for Círculo de Crédito, Mexico's fastest-growing credit bureau—major bet on emerging market credit data as Latin America's fintech ecosystem heats up.
CMBS capital is rotating aggressively into spec data center financing as AI infrastructure buildout reshapes real estate fundamentals; traditional CRE losing institutional mindshare.
ECB mandating banks submit AI cyber-threat mitigation plans—regulatory pressure mounting on how institutions manage risks from advanced AI models embedded in core systems.

PE & Deals.lede: 5 stories tracking LP capital reallocation: mega-funds pivoting to Asia and direct investments, retail capital emerging as a new channel, and secondaries GPs rightsizing for a DPI-constrained market.

bullets.text: Novo Holdings ($36bn AUM) expanding PE GP allocations and pushing into Asia and planetary health—signals institutional appetite for emerging geographies despite macro headwinds.
Temasek admitting EMEA direct investment underperformance, pivoting capital toward AI, private credit, and core-plus infrastructure by 2031—reshuffling of large sovereign capital.
PE GPs courting retail capital as institutional LPs tighten, but structural/regulatory constraints keep channel niche—Azalea's evergreen fund launch shows proof of concept.
Patria closed $677m secondaries fund focusing on smaller deals as DPI issues persist—GPs optimizing fund size to differentiate in a persistently challenged secondaries market.

Markets & Macro.lede: European banking consolidation accelerates while UK capital markets show stark M&A preference over IPOs; meanwhile, technical factors and narrative-driven assets are reshaping credit markets.

bullets.text: UniCredit clinches 48% of Commerzbank after successful tender, positioning for majority control of Germany's second-largest bank despite political pushback from Berlin.
UK M&A bids hit £60bn vs. £2.2bn in new IPOs—a 27:1 ratio that crystallizes capital's flight from primary listings toward consolidation plays.
Credit markets increasingly decoupled from fundamentals: passive flows and algos driving momentum while UK pension funds rotate gilt proceeds into AI-narrative bonds rather than traditional credit.

Newsletters.lede: Four stories span payments disruption, tax planning tailwinds, wealth management tailwinds, and energy sector pressure—plus a note on single-stock ETF proliferation.

bullets.text: Wall Street banks exploring card network acquisitions to dodge interchange fee caps that have cost issuers tens of billions—a structural workaround rather than regulatory fix.
IRS reclassified 'Trump Account' contributions as current-interest gifts, lowering gift-tax exposure and expanding the high-net-worth tax-advantaged toolkit.
UBS Global Wealth Report confirms accelerating wealth concentration driving advisor hiring and AUM growth—tailwind for wealth management franchises.
Saudi Arabia slashing crude prices as Brent hits $72/bbl; signals aggressive positioning and eroding sector pricing power amid broader energy headwinds.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Five stories spanning tech friction points, prediction market applications, land policy shifts, and AI governance questions—with limited direct FIG relevance but decision markets and AI legitimacy worth monitoring.

bullets.text: Prediction markets proved their information aggregation power: a Maine Democratic primary dropout probability swung from 9% to 96% in one day on scandal news. Cowen extends this to broader 'decision markets' that could reshape institutional decision-making.

US coastal cities reclaimed 8% of land from water in the 1890s–1920s but stopped post-WWII despite housing constraints—a stark signal of declined engineering ambition or regulatory tolerance worth examining as real estate pressures mount.

AI governance legitimacy is a live institutional question: applying constitutional theory to Claude's rule sets raises consent and authority issues that FIG firms need to grapple with as AI becomes operationally critical.

all.lede: Four stories on fintech/payments disruption and M&A, plus a technical market warning: Klarna seeks U.S. banking charter, JPM/BofA eye Fiserv's debit network, UniCredit takes control of Commerzbank, and Wall Street banks plot payment network buys to dodge fee caps.

bullets.text: Klarna applies for ILC charter with FDIC—Swedish fintech moving directly into U.S. deposit-taking and lending under regulatory umbrella.
JPMorgan and BofA in talks to acquire Fiserv's debit network; part of broader Wall Street play to own payment infrastructure and escape interchange fee caps.
UniCredit secures 48% Commerzbank stake via tender offer, signals push for majority control of Germany's second-largest bank despite Berlin's political resistance.
FT Markets flags technical decoupling in credit markets—passive flows and algo trading driving momentum over fundamentals; risk of Newton's Cradle effect.
